name: ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018
description: International standard for IT service management systems (SMS). Specifies requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving a service management system. Aligned with ITIL but independently certifiable.
license_note: Metadata only. No ISO copyrighted text reproduced. Clause descriptions are original summaries.

items.title: Understanding the organization and its context
items.description: Requires the organization to determine external and internal issues that are relevant to its purpose and that affect its ability to achieve the intended outcomes of the service management system.
items.purpose: Establish the organizational context for the service management system.

items.title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
items.description: Requires identification of interested parties relevant to the SMS and their requirements, including customers, users, suppliers, regulators, and internal stakeholders.
items.purpose: Identify stakeholders and their requirements relevant to the service management system.

items.title: Determining the scope of the service management system
items.description: Requires the organization to determine the boundaries and applicability of the SMS, considering the services delivered, the organizational units involved, and the locations covered.
items.purpose: Define the scope and boundaries of the service management system.

items.title: Service management system
items.description: Requires the organization to establish, implement, maintain, and continually improve an SMS, including required processes and their interactions, in accordance with the requirements of the standard.
items.purpose: Establish and maintain the service management system with all required processes.

items.title: Leadership and commitment
items.description: Requires top management to demonstrate leadership and commitment with respect to the SMS, including ensuring the SMS policy and objectives are established and compatible with the strategic direction.
items.purpose: Ensure top management demonstrates leadership and commitment to the service management system.

items.title: Policy
items.description: Requires top management to establish an SMS policy that is appropriate to the purpose of the organization, provides a framework for setting objectives, includes commitment to satisfy applicable requirements, and includes commitment to continual improvement.
items.purpose: Establish the service management policy as a framework for objectives and commitment.

items.title: Organizational roles, responsibilities and authorities
items.description: Requires top management to ensure that responsibilities and authorities for relevant roles are assigned and communicated within the organization, including accountability for the SMS.
items.purpose: Assign and communicate roles, responsibilities, and authorities for the SMS.

items.title: Actions to address risks and opportunities
items.description: Requires the organization to determine risks and opportunities that need to be addressed to ensure the SMS can achieve its intended outcomes, prevent or reduce undesired effects, and achieve continual improvement.
items.purpose: Address risks and opportunities that could affect the SMS outcomes.

items.title: Service management objectives and planning to achieve them
items.description: Requires the organization to establish measurable SMS objectives at relevant functions and levels, consistent with the policy, and to plan how to achieve them.
items.purpose: Establish measurable service management objectives and plan their achievement.

items.title: Plan the service management system
items.description: Requires planning of the SMS including creating and maintaining a service management plan that defines the scope, objectives, processes, roles, and resources for the SMS.
items.purpose: Create and maintain a service management plan covering scope, objectives, processes, and resources.

items.title: Resources
items.description: Requires the organization to determine and provide the resources needed for the establishment, implementation, maintenance, and continual improvement of the SMS.
items.purpose: Provide adequate resources for the service management system.

items.title: Competence
items.description: Requires the organization to determine the necessary competence of persons doing work under its control that affects SMS performance, ensure these persons are competent, and retain evidence of competence.
items.purpose: Ensure staff competence for service management activities.

items.title: Awareness
items.description: Requires persons doing work under the organization's control to be aware of the SMS policy, their contribution to the effectiveness of the SMS, and implications of not conforming with SMS requirements.
items.purpose: Ensure awareness of SMS policy and requirements among all relevant personnel.

items.title: Communication
items.description: Requires the organization to determine the internal and external communications relevant to the SMS, including what, when, with whom, and how to communicate.
items.purpose: Establish effective internal and external communication for the SMS.

items.title: Documented information
items.description: Requires the SMS to include documented information required by the standard and determined by the organization as necessary for the effectiveness of the SMS. Covers creation, updating, and control of documented information.
items.purpose: Maintain documented information required for the SMS effectiveness.

items.title: Knowledge
items.description: Requires the organization to determine the knowledge necessary for the operation of its processes and the achievement of results. Knowledge shall be maintained and made available to the extent necessary.
items.purpose: Maintain and share knowledge necessary for SMS operation.

items.title: Operational planning and control
items.description: Requires the organization to plan, implement, and control the processes needed to meet requirements and to implement actions for risks and opportunities. Covers managing planned changes and unintended changes.
items.purpose: Plan and control operational processes to meet service management requirements.

items.title: Service portfolio
items.description: Requires the organization to manage a service portfolio that includes all services, their status, and lifecycle stage. The portfolio supports decision making about service investments and resource allocation.
items.purpose: Manage the service portfolio to support service investment decisions.

items.title: Plan the services
items.description: Requires planning of new or changed services, including determining requirements, resources, dependencies, and acceptance criteria before changes are implemented.
items.purpose: Plan new or changed services with clear requirements and acceptance criteria.

items.title: Control of parties involved in the service lifecycle
items.description: Requires the organization to control other parties involved in the service lifecycle, including suppliers, customers, and internal groups. Covers responsibilities and interfaces between parties.
items.purpose: Control and coordinate all parties involved in service delivery.

items.title: Service catalogue management
items.description: Requires the organization to create and maintain a service catalogue containing information about each service and the services available to customers. The catalogue must be kept current.
items.purpose: Maintain an accurate and current service catalogue.

items.title: Asset management
items.description: Requires the organization to manage assets including IT infrastructure, applications, and information required for service delivery. Covers asset lifecycle from procurement through disposal.
items.purpose: Manage IT assets throughout their lifecycle to support service delivery.

items.title: Configuration management
items.description: Requires the organization to define configuration item types and manage configuration information including relationships between items, baselines, and configuration audits.
items.purpose: Manage configuration information including relationships and baselines.

items.title: Relationship and agreement management
items.description: Requires identifying service relationships and managing them through formal agreements. Covers customer, supplier, and internal relationships with defined interfaces and responsibilities.
items.purpose: Manage service relationships through formal agreements with clear responsibilities.

items.title: Business relationship management
items.description: Requires identification and management of customers and business stakeholders, understanding their needs, and ensuring regular communication and satisfaction measurement.
items.purpose: Manage business relationships to ensure customer needs are understood and met.

items.title: Service level management
items.description: Requires defining, agreeing, documenting, and managing service levels through service level agreements. Covers monitoring, reporting, and reviewing service levels against agreed targets.
items.purpose: Define and manage service level agreements to meet agreed service quality.

items.title: Supplier management
items.description: Requires managing suppliers including defining requirements, evaluating and selecting suppliers, managing contracts, and monitoring supplier performance against agreed commitments.
items.purpose: Manage supplier relationships and contracts to ensure service delivery requirements are met.

items.title: Budgeting and accounting for services
items.description: Requires budgeting and accounting for services, including forecasting costs, tracking expenditure, and ensuring financial resources are available for service delivery and improvement.
items.purpose: Manage financial resources for service delivery through effective budgeting and accounting.

items.title: Demand management
items.description: Requires the organization to determine current and anticipated demand for services, including patterns and trends, and to manage demand in conjunction with capacity management.
items.purpose: Understand and manage current and anticipated demand for services.

items.title: Capacity management
items.description: Requires determining current and expected capacity requirements and managing capacity to meet service demands. Covers capacity planning, monitoring, and optimization.
items.purpose: Ensure adequate capacity to meet current and expected service demands.

items.title: Change management
items.description: Requires a formal change management policy and process covering all changes to services and service components. Includes change assessment, authorization, implementation, and review.
items.purpose: Manage all changes in a controlled manner to minimize risk and disruption.

items.title: Change management - design and transition of new or changed services
items.description: Requires planning and managing the design and transition of new or changed services, including requirements, design, build, test, and deployment activities.
items.purpose: Plan and control the design and transition of new or changed services.

items.title: Release and deployment management
items.description: Requires planning and managing the release and deployment of services and service components into the live environment, including release criteria, deployment methods, and verification.
items.purpose: Plan and control releases and deployments into the live environment.

items.title: Incident management
items.description: Requires a process for managing all incidents including detection, recording, classification, prioritization, investigation, diagnosis, resolution, and closure. Includes major incident procedures.
items.purpose: Restore normal service operation as quickly as possible and minimize business impact.

items.title: Service request management
items.description: Requires a process for managing service requests from logging through fulfillment. Covers standard request models, approval procedures, and fulfillment tracking.
items.purpose: Fulfill service requests effectively and efficiently in accordance with agreed procedures.

items.title: Problem management
items.description: Requires a process for identifying, analyzing, and managing problems to reduce the impact and likelihood of incidents. Covers root cause analysis, workarounds, and known errors.
items.purpose: Reduce the impact and likelihood of incidents through root cause analysis and resolution.

items.title: Service availability management
items.description: Requires monitoring and managing service availability to ensure agreed availability targets are met. Covers availability planning, measurement, and improvement of availability levels.
items.purpose: Ensure services are available at the levels agreed with customers.

items.title: Service continuity management
items.description: Requires maintaining service continuity plans that are tested and updated. Covers business impact analysis, continuity strategy, plan development, testing, and maintenance.
items.purpose: Ensure service continuity in the event of major disruptions or disasters.

items.title: Information security management
items.description: Requires implementing and maintaining information security controls to protect information confidentiality, integrity, and availability. Covers security policies, risk assessment, access controls, and security incident management.
items.purpose: Protect information assets through appropriate security controls and risk management.

items.title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
items.description: Requires the organization to determine what needs to be monitored and measured, the methods used, when monitoring and measuring shall be performed, and when results shall be analyzed and evaluated.
items.purpose: Monitor and measure SMS performance and evaluate results for improvement.

items.title: Internal audit
items.description: Requires conducting internal audits at planned intervals to provide information on whether the SMS conforms to the organization's own requirements and the requirements of the standard.
items.purpose: Conduct internal audits to verify SMS conformance and effectiveness.

items.title: Management review
items.description: Requires top management to review the SMS at planned intervals to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness. Reviews consider audit results, customer feedback, performance metrics, and improvement opportunities.
items.purpose: Ensure continuing suitability and effectiveness of the SMS through top management review.

items.title: Service reporting
items.description: Requires the production of service reports that include performance against service level targets, information about significant events, workload and resource utilization data, and trend analysis.
items.purpose: Produce service reports that support decision making and demonstrate service performance.

items.title: Nonconformity and corrective action
items.description: Requires the organization to react to nonconformities, evaluate the need for action to eliminate causes, implement corrective actions, and review effectiveness of corrective actions taken.
items.purpose: Address nonconformities and take corrective action to prevent recurrence.

items.title: Continual improvement
items.description: Requires the organization to continually improve the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the SMS. Covers identifying improvement opportunities, prioritizing improvements, and implementing improvement actions.
items.purpose: Continually improve the service management system and its outcomes.
